A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT DAMAGE IN THE CROSS PLAINS AREA OF ROBERTSON COUNTY WAS DUE TO STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. A FEW LARGE LIMBS ON SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND ROOF DAMAGE TO SOME OUTHOUSES WERE OBSERVED. ONE SUCH LARGE OUTHOUSE WAS COMPLETELY REMOVED OF ITS ALUMINUM ROOFING. NEARBY OUTHOUSES SAW MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. A MAXIMUM WIND GUST OF 77 MPH WAS ESTIMATED DUE TO THE EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE OBSERVED.

NEARBY OUTHOUSES SAW MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. A MAXIMUM WIND GUST OF 93 MPH WAS ESTIMATED DUE TO THE EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE OBSERVED.
